questions.q: What is the primary objective of staffing in an organization?
questions.answer: The primary objective of staffing is to ensure that the organization has the right people in the right jobs at the right time to achieve its goals and objectives.

questions.q: Which of the following is a source of recruitment? (Choose the correct option)
Explain the internal sources of recruitment. Give two examples.
questions.answer: Internal sources of recruitment refer to the sources within the organization. Examples include promotions and transfers.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The process of _______________ involves a systematic and comprehensive analysis of the job requirements and the qualities required for the job.
State one advantage of internal recruitment.
questions.answer: One advantage of internal recruitment is that it boosts the morale of existing employees and motivates them to work harder.

questions.q: What is the difference between recruitment and selection? Give two points.
questions.answer: Recruitment refers to the process of finding and attracting candidates for a job opening, while selection involves choosing the best candidate for the job. Two key differences are: (i) recruitment is a wider process, while selection is a narrower process; (ii) recruitment is a positive process, while selection is a negative process.

questions.q: True or False: Training is a part of the staffing function.
Explain the concept of downsizing. What are its advantages and disadvantages?
questions.answer: Downsizing refers to the reduction of the workforce in an organization. Advantages include cost savings and increased efficiency, while disadvantages include loss of talent and decreased morale.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a job description?
questions.answer: The purpose of a job description is to outline the key responsibilities, duties, and requirements of a job.

questions.q: Choose the correct answer: Which of the following is a type of selection test? (Choose the correct option)
